title: Smoky Broiled Tunisian Mushrooms Soup
description: A fermented, light Tunisian soup bursting with smoky flavors from broiled mushrooms and aromatic spices, sugar‑free and easy to whip up.
instructions: Preheat the broiler to high and line a baking sheet with foil.
In a large bowl, toss mushroom slices with 1 tbsp olive oil, smoked paprika, cumin, coriander, salt, and pepper until evenly coated.
Arrange the mushrooms in a single layer on the prepared baking sheet and broil for 4–5 minutes, turning once, until edges are caramelized and slightly charred.
While the mushrooms are broiling, heat the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat and sauté onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in tomato paste and cook, stirring, for 1 minute to release its aroma.
Pour in vegetable broth, add the broiled mushrooms, and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer; cook for 8–10 minutes to meld flavors.
Finish with lemon juice, adjust seasoning, and serve hot garnished with chopped parsley.
